title: Contributing
content: \<Contributing components={{ h1: () => null }} />

title: Events
content: *"Events Hooks"* provide powerful abstractions for handling Lynx events.
description: "Events Hooks" provide powerful abstractions for handling Lynx events.

title: useEventListener
description: A hook that helps you add a global event listener as early as possible.

title: useExposureForNode
description: Node-level exposure hook with optional admission gating.

title: useExposureForPage
description: Page-level exposure hook that consumes GlobalEventEmitter events and manages multiple items with admission gating.

title: useStayTime
description: Track how long an element stays visible, with optional manual control.

title: Guide
content: This page has been replaced by the new overview page: [/overview]().

title: Lifecycle
content:
> **Credit:** Documentation based on [react-use]()

*"Lifecycle Hooks"* modify and extend built-in React hooks or imitate React Class component lifecycle patterns.
description: Credit: Documentation based on react-use "Lifecycle Hooks" modify and extend built-in React hooks or imitate React Class component lifecycle patterns.

title: useEffectOnce
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React lifecycle hook that runs an effect only once.

title: useLifecycles
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React lifecycle hook that call mount and unmount callbacks, when component is mounted and un-mounted, respectively.

title: useMountedState
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use NOTE!: despite having State in its name this hook does not cause component re-render.This component designed to be used to avoid state updates on unmounted components. Lifecycle hook providing ability to check component's mount state.Returns a function that will return true if component mounted and false otherwise.

title: useUnmount
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React lifecycle hook that calls a function when the component will unmount. Use useLifecycles if you need both a mount and unmount function.

title: useUnmountPromise
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use A life-cycle hook that provides a higher order promise that does not resolve if component un-mounts.

title: useUpdateEffect
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React effect hook that ignores the first invocation (e.g. on mount). The signature is exactly the same as the useEffect hook.

title: Main Thread Script (MTS)
content: *"MTS Hooks"* unleash the power of Lynx's [Main Thread Script]().
description: "MTS Hooks" unleash the power of Lynx's Main Thread Script.

title: useMainThreadImperativeHandle
description:
A Hook for exposing imperative handles to parent components on the main-thread. This is the main-thread version of
React's useImperativeHandle, allowing you to trigger component methods that run on the main-thread.

title: usePointerEvent
description: A Hook unifies TouchEvent and MouseEvent into PointerEvent, to help with handling pointer events in a cross-platform manner.

title: useTapLock
description: A Hook for locking tap events when tap movement exceeds a specified threshold in a given direction.

title: useTouchEmulation
description: A Hook synthesizes TouchEvent from MouseEvent, so existing touch handlers can work seamlessly with mouse input. Useful for migration from touch-only code.

title: useVelocity
description: A hook that tracks touch velocity and direction with smoothing support.

title: Overview
content: This page is auto-generated from the docs sidebar. Add a new `.md` file under `docs/en` and it will appear here automatically.

title: Side Effects
content:
> **Credit:** Documentation based on [react-use]()

*"Side-effect Hooks"* allow your app trigger various side-effects using browser's API.
description: Credit: Documentation based on react-use "Side-effect Hooks" allow your app trigger various side-effects using browser's API.

title: useDebounce
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React hook that delays invoking a function until after wait milliseconds have elapsed since the last time the debounced function was invoked. The third argument is the array of values that the debounce depends on, in the same manner as useEffect. The debounce timeout will start when one of the values changes.

title: useError
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React side-effect hook that returns an error dispatcher.

title: useThrottle
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React hooks that throttle.

title: useThrottleFn
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React hooks that throttle.

title: State
content:
> **Credit:** Documentation based on [react-use]()

*"State Hooks"* allow you to easily manage state of booleans, arrays, and maps.
description: Credit: Documentation based on react-use "State Hooks" allow you to easily manage state of booleans, arrays, and maps.

title: createMemo
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use Hook factory, receives a function to be memoized, returns a memoized React hook, which receives the same arguments and returns the same result as the original function.

title: useBoolean
content:
> **Credit:** Re-export from [react-use]()

React state hook that tracks value of a boolean.

`useBoolean` is an alias for `useToggle`.

## Usage

See [useToggle]() for more details.

## Type Declarations

```ts
declare function useBoolean(
  initialValue: boolean
): [boolean, (nextValue?: any) => void];
```
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React state hook that tracks value of a boolean. useBoolean is an alias for useToggle.

title: useCounter
content:
> **Credit:** Re-export from [react-use]()

React state hook that tracks a numeric value.

## Usage

```tsx


const Demo = () => {
  const [min, { inc: incMin, dec: decMin }] = useCounter(1);
  const [max, { inc: incMax, dec: decMax }] = useCounter(10);
  const [value, { inc, dec, set, reset }] = useCounter(5, max, min);

  return (
    <view>
      <view>
        <text>
          current: {value} [min: {min}; max: {max}]
        </text>
      </view>
      <view>
        <text>Current value: </text>
        <view bindtap={() => inc()}>Increment</view>
        <view bindtap={() => dec()}>Decrement</view>
        <view bindtap={() => inc(5)}>Increment (+5)</view>
        <view bindtap={() => dec(5)}>Decrement (-5)</view>
        <view bindtap={() => set(100)}>Set 100</view>
        <view bindtap={() => reset()}>Reset</view>
        <view bindtap={() => reset(25)}>Reset (25)</view>
      </view>
      <view>
        <text> Min value:</text>
        <view bindtap={() => incMin()}>Increment</view>
        <view bindtap={() => decMin()}>Decrement</view>
      </view>
      <view>
        <text> Max value:</text>
        <view bindtap={() => incMax()}>Increment</view>
        <view bindtap={() => decMax()}>Decrement</view>
      </view>
    </view>
  );
};
```

## Type Declarations

```ts
declare function useCounter(
  initialValue?: IHookStateInitAction<number>,
  max?: number | null,
  min?: number | null
): [number, CounterActions];
```
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React state hook that tracks a numeric value.

title: useDefault
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React state hook that returns the default value when state is null or undefined.

title: useImmer
description: Credit: Inspired by use-immer Manage complex nested state with ease using immer. Think of useImmer as useState but supercharged: you can modify state directly (like state.count++) without worrying about immutability rules. Learn more about Immer: https://github.com/immerjs/immer

title: useLatest
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React state hook that returns the latest state as described in the React hooks FAQ. This is mostly useful to get access to the latest value of some props or state inside an asynchronous callback, instead of that value at the time the callback was created from.

title: useMap
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React state hook that tracks a value of an object.

title: useNumber
content:
> **Credit:** Re-export from [react-use]()

React state hook that tracks a numeric value.

`useNumber` is an alias for `useCounter`.

## Usage

See [useCounter]() for more details.

## Type Declarations

```ts
declare function useNumber(
  initialValue?: IHookStateInitAction<number>,
  max?: number | null,
  min?: number | null
): [number, CounterActions];
```
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React state hook that tracks a numeric value. useNumber is an alias for useCounter.

title: usePrevious
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React state hook that returns the previous state as described in the React hooks FAQ.

title: useQueue
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React state hook implements simple FIFO queue.

title: useSet
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React state hook that tracks a Set.

title: useSetState
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React state hook that creates setState method which works similar to how this.setState works in class components—it merges object changes into current state.

title: useToggle
description: Credit: Re-export from react-use React state hook that tracks value of a boolean.

title: useUniqueId
description: React hook that generates a unique ID. The ID is stable across re-renders.
